summary: Wired iOS doc-as-test as the 5th gate signal. Added IOS_DAT_* constants, run_ios_doc_as_test_check() with swiftc availability guard, updated print_summary/main/quiet output with ios_* fields. Updated CI_GATE.md: 4-platform coverage, iOS macOS requirement, Phase 2.2 runner note. Added binary-cache/ to .gitignore. Gate runs all five checks and exits 0.

notes.gitignore: binary-cache/ added; all other iOS artifacts already covered (extracted/*.swift, _manifest.json, .build/, .swiftpm/, Package.resolved, vendor/). No tracked files required untracking — no leak from prior tasks.
notes.gate_run_output: Regex drift: baseline=0 candidate=0 delta=0 ✓ | TS: passed=72 skipped=4 blocking=0 ✓ | Flutter: passed=41 skipped=3 blocking=0 ✓ | Android: passed=70 skipped=9 warnings=4 blocking=0 ✓ | iOS: passed=66 skipped=7 warnings=66 blocking=0 ✓ | PASS
notes.unavailable_branch: If swiftc is not on PATH (Linux), run_ios_doc_as_test_check() returns available=False with crash_reason='swiftc not found — install Xcode CLI tools'. print_summary prints 'unavailable (swiftc not found ...)' and the verdict is not affected — only available+!crashed+blocking>0 contributes to overall_fail.
